title: Fixing React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Object References

statement.markdown: Write a React memoized component that renders a list of items. The component accepts an array of objects with a unique identifier and a name. The component should render the list of items, but it should only re-render when the array of objects or the object references within the array change.
statement.inputFormat: An array of objects with a unique identifier and a name.
statement.notes: The component should only re-render when the array of objects or the object references within the array change.

constraints.description: The number of items in the array.
The number of unique identifiers in the array.

examples.input: [{ id: 1, name: 'Item 1' }, { id: 2, name: 'Item 2' }, { id: 3, name: 'Item 3' }]
examples.explanation: The component should re-render when the array of objects changes.

examples.input: [{ id: 1, name: 'Item 1' }, { id: 2, name: 'Item 2' }, { id: 3, name: 'Item 3' }]
examples.explanation: The component should not re-render when the object references within the array do not change.

hints.text: Use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the array of objects.
Compare the memoized array with the new array to determine if the component should re-render.

editorial.title: Fixing React Memoized Component Re-renders Due to Changing Object References
editorial.intuition: The component should only re-render when the array of objects or the object references within the array change.
editorial.bruteForce: Compare the entire array of objects on every render to determine if the component should re-render.
editorial.betterSolution: Use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the array of objects and compare it with the new array to determine if the component should re-render.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the `useMemo` hook with a custom comparison function to memoize the array of objects and compare it with the new array to determine if the component should re-render.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The component should only re-render when the array of objects or the object references within the array change.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run to test the correct implementation.
commonMistakes: Not using the `useMemo` hook to memoize the array of objects.
Not comparing the memoized array with the new array to determine if the component should re-render.

testCases.input: [{ id: 1, name: 'Item 1' }, { id: 2, name: 'Item 2' }, { id: 3, name: 'Item 3' }]
[{ id: 1, name: 'Item 1' }, { id: 2, name: 'Item 2' }, { id: 3, name: 'Item 3' }]
